critic_raw: The prompt is explicitly truncated and self-describes as matching a 'corrupted_narrative' — there is no complete methodology to evaluate. The visible fragment describes a plausible experimental design (transformer fine-tuning vs. BM25 on IT tickets, evaluated by MRR with temporal controls), but the actual design choices — data splitting strategy, fine-tuning details, label noise handling, boilerplate filtering, statistical test selection, and baseline configuration — are all in the missing portion. Critiquing the fragment would mean critiquing a hypothesis statement, not a methodology. I cannot identify genuine flaws in design choices that are not present. The truncation is the only real issue, and it is not a methodological flaw — it is a missing document.
